Text: A cheaper early mine for Fulgur Fangs. It can run in a barebones mode without additives or in a boosted mode using extract and planks.

Name of the electrified Fulgur Fangs mine

Name of the test house with optional electricity

Text: A test Fulgur Fangs dwelling. It still works as a normal home, but when connected to the electric grid it consumes 20 kW and improves resident comfort.
Comment: Description of the test house with optional electricity

Text: Light in the window and a bit more comfort.
Comment: Flavor text for the test electric house
Name of the first real electric Fulgur Fangs sawmill

Text: The first real electric workshop for Fulgur Fangs. It works inside a distributor area, draws 100 kW, and currently reuses the Iron Teeth industrial lumber mill shell as placeholder visuals.

Text: One saw is good. An electric drive is better.

Mechanical to electric converter name

Text: An early bridge between mechanical power and the Fulgur Fangs electric grid. Pulls rotation from shafts and feeds up to 500 kW into the wire network.
Comment: Mechanical to electric converter description
Advanced mechanical to electric converter name

Text: A heavy-duty converter for industrial districts. Draws up to 2000 mechanical power and feeds it into the electric network.
Comment: Advanced mechanical to electric converter description
Hydroelectric valve name

Text: A test hydro plant for Fulgur Fangs built on the Iron Teeth valve shell. It keeps the vanilla flow controls, generates up to 500 kW from real water flow, and supplies the grid through a distributor area like an accumulator.
Comment: Hydroelectric valve description
Large custom hydro plant name

Text: A larger 3x2x2 Fulgur Fangs hydro prototype built on a custom model. It works as a multi-cell valve: native water simulation passes through a wide spillway, one shared gate regulates the flow, and the building can generate up to 1500 kW through distributor coverage.
Comment: Large custom hydro plant description
Upper-tier hydro plant test name

Text: A test version of the large 3x2x2 hydro body that keeps only the upper valve row active. The lower tier is sealed by a hidden half-wall so you can test how an upper-only flow level affects throughput and generation.
Comment: Upper-tier hydro plant test description
Electric pole name

Text: The basic support for local transmission lines. Carries electricity for 20 m, only supports a straight pass-through, and loses 10 kW while the line is actively serving consumers.
Comment: Electric pole description
Splitter name

Text: A compact junction for dense electrical layouts. Works over short distance and can branch the network in up to four directions.
Comment: Splitter description
Transmission tower name

Text: A long-distance backbone tower for moving power between districts. Carries electricity for 200 m and loses 30 kW under active load.
Comment: Transmission tower description
Distributor name

Text: A local substation for neighborhood supply. Connects to the wire network, creates a powered service area, and feeds nearby electric buildings.
Comment: Distributor description
Accumulator name

Text: A buffer for the local electric grid. It does not connect by wire directly: instead it works inside a distributor's service area, stores up to 20000 kWh, and supports the district during shortages.
Comment: Accumulator description
Temporary electric consumer name

Text: A technical consumer for validating the electric grid. Useful for quickly checking connectivity, losses, and delivered power in-game.
Comment: Temporary electric consumer description
